title: About
text:
I am a motivated and dependable individual with strong communication and creative skills, currently pursuing higher education. A proven ability to manage projects and adapt to new challenges. Seeking opportunities to contribute to a dynamic team while continuing to grow both personally and professionally.

Throughout my time at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln, I have acquired various skills, including proficiency in Adobe Photoshop and Lightroom, as well as experience with social media platforms such as Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook. Additionally, I have developed a solid understanding of design principles, including color theory, typography, and layout. I managed various social media accounts throughout high school and college, developing many of the skills I have acquired.
I have done graphic design work for Nebraska, as well photography.

title: Why Caleb Williams is Scarier than you think.
text:
Caleb Williams, the 23-year-old Chicago Bears Quarterback, is WAY scarier than you think.









Now, why would I show you this? Why is it cool to brag about being second on the all-time list, especially it being a list of bears quarterbacks, who are historically bad.

As mediocre as Jay Cutler was, did you know that the NFL’s passing numbers have gone down significantly since his time as the Bears’ Quarterback?










So Caleb Williams is already off to a promising start despite being second place behind Cutler—so far. But what does that really mean? Brett Favre was the all-time passing leader at one point, and while he was a legend. He is a prime example of other things you would NOT like in a quarterback.
In Favre’s career, he was known as a “gunslinger.” Someone with a rocket attached to his right arm and can make some crazy throws, but also someone who puts the ball into harm’s way a lot. Now, does Caleb Williams have this same problem with turning the ball over? Let’s find out.









Caleb Williams already has one of the highest Touchdown to Interception Ratios of all-time, yes you read that right, all-time.

title: Has Ben Johnson saved the bears?
text:
How has Ben Johnson quote on quote “saved the bears?”









You can see that there is a substantial jump in the amount of points scored since Ben Johnson has arrived.









Sure, it isn’t the highest on the list, but 140 yards per game is still pretty darn good, and the biggest season was the Justin Fields year when the bears had one of the top rushing attacks in the league. But we know that rushing isn’t the only thing that matters to an offense, so let’s see more.









So we can see that in 2025, Ben Johnson created one of the most all-around complete offenses that the Bears have had in quite a while.
